narrative.headline: 2019 Chevrolet Traverse: power train tops owner complaints
narrative.summary: From 391 owner complaints filed with NHTSA, the power train stands out by far, named in 40% of reports (158), followed by the electrical system at 27% (106) and the engine at 16% (62). A handful of these reports involved fires and crashes, and a few owners were injured. Read each area below to know what to watch for.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The single most-reported area: 158 owners (40% of all complaints) flagged power train trouble. Reports include 2 fires and 2 crashes, with 1 person injured. The biggest cluster to ask a mechanic about before buying.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The second most-reported area: 106 owners (27%) reported electrical problems. These reports include 2 crashes and 2 injuries. Worth a careful check of warning lights and electrical functions on a test drive.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 62 owners (16%) reported engine trouble, including 2 fires and 2 injuries. A meaningful third cluster, so confirm the engine runs cleanly and holds idle.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 25 owners (6%) reported fuel and propulsion system issues. No fires, crashes, or injuries are noted in these reports, but it is still worth knowing about.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 20 owners (5%) reported brake problems. Smaller in number but safety-relevant: these reports include 2 crashes and 1 injury, so test the brakes thoroughly.
